step_description: Statistics staleness scan across all user tables
rows: 20% threshold = auto would need 2.4M mods — DID NOT TRIGGER
20% threshold = auto would need 5.6M mods — DID NOT TRIGGER
Within auto threshold — but data skew is high
CRITICAL — 576% modification rate since last update
narrative_hint: Two critical cases: AuditLog at 576% modification rate since last update (54 days ago) and Orders at 250% (47 days). Both have grown so much since statistics were last updated that the histograms bear no resemblance to the current data distribution. The auto-update threshold was never triggered because the 20% rule is calculated against the rows AT THE TIME of the last update — not the current row count. For a table that grew from 12M to 42M rows, the threshold was 2.4M modifications but 30M occurred before it reset. This is the well-known auto-stats threshold problem on large tables. Claude should identify both as critical and proceed to cardinality estimate errors to see the query impact.
